# crc32

```go
import "hash/crc32"
```

Package crc32 implements the 32\-bit cyclic redundancy check, or CRC\-32, checksum. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cyclic_redundancy_check for information.

Polynomials are represented in LSB\-first form also known as reversed representation.

See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mathematics_of_cyclic_redundancy_checks#Reversed_representations_and_reciprocal_polynomials for information.

## Constants

<a name="IEEE"></a>Predefined polynomials.

```go
const (
    // IEEE is by far and away the most common CRC-32 polynomial.
    // Used by ethernet (IEEE 802.3), v.42, fddi, gzip, zip, png, ...
    IEEE = 0xedb88320

    // Castagnoli's polynomial, used in iSCSI.
    // Has better error detection characteristics than IEEE.
    // https://dx.doi.org/10.1109/26.231911
    Castagnoli = 0x82f63b78

    // Koopman's polynomial.
    // Also has better error detection characteristics than IEEE.
    // https://dx.doi.org/10.1109/DSN.2002.1028931
    Koopman = 0xeb31d82e
)
```

<a name="Size"></a>The size of a CRC\-32 checksum in bytes.

```go
const Size = 4
```

## Variables

<a name="IEEETable"></a>IEEETable is the table for the [IEEE](<#IEEE>) polynomial.

```go
var IEEETable = simpleMakeTable(IEEE)
```

<a name="Checksum"></a>

## func [Checksum](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/hash/crc32/crc32.go#L239>)

```go
func Checksum(data []byte, tab *Table) uint32
```

Checksum returns the CRC\-32 checksum of data using the polynomial represented by the [Table](<#Table>).

<a name="ChecksumIEEE"></a>

## func [ChecksumIEEE](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/hash/crc32/crc32.go#L243>)

```go
func ChecksumIEEE(data []byte) uint32
```

ChecksumIEEE returns the CRC\-32 checksum of data using the [IEEE](<#IEEE>) polynomial.

<a name="New"></a>

## func [New](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/hash/crc32/crc32.go#L146>)

```go
func New(tab *Table) hash.Hash32
```

New creates a new [hash.Hash32](<https://pkg.go.dev/hash/#Hash32>) computing the CRC\-32 checksum using the polynomial represented by the [Table](<#Table>). Its Sum method will lay the value out in big\-endian byte order. The returned Hash32 also implements [encoding.BinaryMarshaler](<https://pkg.go.dev/encoding/#BinaryMarshaler>) and [encoding.BinaryUnmarshaler](<https://pkg.go.dev/encoding/#BinaryUnmarshaler>) to marshal and unmarshal the internal state of the hash.

<a name="NewIEEE"></a>

## func [NewIEEE](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/hash/crc32/crc32.go#L158>)

```go
func NewIEEE() hash.Hash32
```

NewIEEE creates a new [hash.Hash32](<https://pkg.go.dev/hash/#Hash32>) computing the CRC\-32 checksum using the [IEEE](<#IEEE>) polynomial. Its Sum method will lay the value out in big\-endian byte order. The returned Hash32 also implements [encoding.BinaryMarshaler](<https://pkg.go.dev/encoding/#BinaryMarshaler>) and [encoding.BinaryUnmarshaler](<https://pkg.go.dev/encoding/#BinaryUnmarshaler>) to marshal and unmarshal the internal state of the hash.

<a name="Update"></a>

## func [Update](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/hash/crc32/crc32.go#L217>)

```go
func Update(crc uint32, tab *Table, p []byte) uint32
```

Update returns the result of adding the bytes in p to the crc.

<a name="Table"></a>

## type [Table](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/hash/crc32/crc32.go#L44>)

Table is a 256\-word table representing the polynomial for efficient processing.

```go
type Table [256]uint32
```

<a name="MakeTable"></a>

### func [MakeTable](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/hash/crc32/crc32.go#L122>)

```go
func MakeTable(poly uint32) *Table
```

MakeTable returns a [Table](<#Table>) constructed from the specified polynomial. The contents of this [Table](<#Table>) must not be modified.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"hash/crc32"
)

func main() {
	// In this package, the CRC polynomial is represented in reversed notation,
	// or LSB-first representation.
	//
	// LSB-first representation is a hexadecimal number with n bits, in which the
	// most significant bit represents the coefficient of x⁰ and the least significant
	// bit represents the coefficient of xⁿ⁻¹ (the coefficient for xⁿ is implicit).
	//
	// For example, CRC32-Q, as defined by the following polynomial,
	//	x³²+ x³¹+ x²⁴+ x²²+ x¹⁶+ x¹⁴+ x⁸+ x⁷+ x⁵+ x³+ x¹+ x⁰
	// has the reversed notation 0b11010101100000101000001010000001, so the value
	// that should be passed to MakeTable is 0xD5828281.
	crc32q := crc32.MakeTable(0xD5828281)
	fmt.Printf("%08x\n", crc32.Checksum([]byte("Hello world"), crc32q))
}
```

#### Output

```go
2964d064
```

</p>
</details>
